step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard and the transcript, the architecture represents Grindr's next-generation chat system which handles 121 billion chats annually. I will define: 1. A User node representing the mobile app interface. 2. Two distinct EKS clusters/nodes as drawn: 'Amazon EKS REST and WebSocket' handling connection layer, and 'Amazon EKS Chat and Notifications' as the core logic brain. 3. DynamoDB for storing scale-heavy chat bodies. 4. Aurora Postgres for relational metadata/inbox headers. 5. ElastiCache for Redis caching inboxes. 6. Amazon Pinpoint for push notifications when offline. 7. Amazon MSK for streaming metadata events to decoupled consumer microservices (like spam filters).

graph.name: Grindr Next-Gen Chat Architecture
graph.notes: Handles 121 billion annual chats. Bifurcates write-heavy chat bodies to DynamoDB (encrypted via KMS) and read-heavy inbox metadata to Aurora/Redis cache. Utilizes MSK for decoupled async operations.

nodes.name: Grindr Mobile App
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles WebSocket (chats) and REST (inbox queries) requests.

nodes.name: EKS REST & WebSocket
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Manages active WebSocket connections (W1, W2) and basic validation.

nodes.name: EKS Chat & Notifications
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Central microservice brain orchestrating logic, security, and persistence.

nodes.name: ElastiCache Redis
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Caches active user inboxes to prevent direct Aurora DB reads.

nodes.name: Amazon Aurora Postgres
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores read-heavy inbox metadata, timestamps, and message headers.

nodes.name: Amazon DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Scalable key-value store for encrypted application chat bodies.

nodes.name: Amazon Pinpoint
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Triggers mobile push alerts over telephony network when user is offline.

nodes.name: Amazon MSK
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Managed Kafka cluster decoupling async flows like spam protection.

edges.notes: Forwards message to chat processing services.
Publishes event to Kafka for offline processing.
Checks if recipient user is online.
If online, pushes chat directly over recipient socket.
If offline, routes push request to Pinpoint.
Sends push notification alert to device.
User requests inbox retrieval over REST.
Queries Redis cache for user's inbox.
On cache miss, loads from Aurora database and backfills Redis.
